description: Pass/fail checklist for all Vapi call scripts before deployment. Echo reviews for quality, naturalness, and effectiveness. Shield handles legal compliance separately.

verdict_rules.pass: All items pass → proceed to Shield compliance check
verdict_rules.fail: Any item fails → return to Scribe with specific notes. Fix and re-review.
verdict_rules.note: Echo focuses on quality and effectiveness. Shield handles TCPA/legal. Both must pass.

criteria.label: AI Disclosure in First 15 Seconds
criteria.description: The script must disclose AI nature within the first 15 seconds of the call — roughly the first 37 words at 150 WPM. This is both a quality standard and a legal requirement (also checked by Shield).
criteria.pass_condition: AI disclosure appears in the OPENING section within the first 37 words.
criteria.measurement: Count words in OPENING section before disclosure. Must be <= 37 words before 'AI', 'artificial intelligence', 'virtual assistant', or 'not a human' appears.
fail_conditions: Disclosure appears after word 37 in opening
Disclosure is only in a separate 'disclaimer' note not part of the spoken script
No disclosure present
automated_checks: Extract OPENING section
Count words to first occurrence of AI disclosure keywords
Flag if word count > 37 before disclosure
Flag if disclosure keywords not found in OPENING at all

criteria.label: Opening Sounds Natural
criteria.description: The opening lines must sound like natural spoken language. Not robotic, not stiff, not reading from a brochure. Should pass the 'read-aloud test' — if it sounds weird said out loud, it needs work.
criteria.pass_condition: Opening reads naturally when spoken aloud. Uses conversational phrasing. Appropriate greeting for time of day. Doesn't sound like a press release.
fail_conditions: Robotic phrasing: 'I am calling to inform you that...'
Corporate stiffness: 'We are pleased to offer you the opportunity to...'
Over-formal: 'Dear valued business owner, I am reaching out today...'
Breathless sales opener with no human warmth
Starts with a false premise: 'We spoke last week...' (if untrue)
criteria.naturalness_test: Read the opening aloud. Would a normal person say this on the phone? If you'd feel embarrassed saying it, it fails.
good_openers: Hey [Name], this is Aria from PantryMate — I'm an AI assistant. Quick question, do you have 90 seconds?
Hi [Name], I'm calling from PantryMate — just so you know, I'm an AI. I'm reaching out because...
automated_checks: Flag formal/robotic phrase patterns: 'I am calling to inform', 'please be advised', 'we are pleased to',  'valued customer'
Flag if opening is over 60 words (too long before getting to the point)
Flag use of passive voice in first 3 sentences
Check for false continuity phrases: 'as discussed', 'per our conversation', 'following up' on cold call scripts

criteria.label: Objection Handlers Are Realistic
criteria.description: The script must include at least 3 objection handlers. Each must respond to the objection genuinely — not dismiss it, not steamroll it, not use pushy counters. The handler should acknowledge the concern and offer a real response.
criteria.pass_condition: Script contains OBJECTION_HANDLERS section with at least 3 distinct objections, each with a non-dismissive, realistic response.
expected_objections_to_cover: I need to think about it
fail_conditions: Fewer than 3 objection handlers
Handlers that dismiss the objection: 'I understand but...' immediately pivoting without acknowledgment
Pushy counters: 'But you really should consider...' or 'Think about what you're leaving on the table'
Fake urgency in objection responses: 'We only have 2 spots left so you should decide now'
Handlers that just repeat the pitch
No OBJECTION_HANDLERS section
automated_checks: Find OBJECTION_HANDLERS section
Count distinct objection entries
Flag if count < 3
Scan objection responses for dismissive phrases: 'but anyway', 'regardless', 'the thing is though'
Scan for pressure phrases in objection handlers: 'you need to', 'you should really', 'don't miss'

criteria.label: Cold Outreach Calls Under 3 Minutes
criteria.description: For cold outreach scripts, the full script (excluding objection handler branches) must estimate to under 3 minutes speaking time at 150 WPM. That's 450 words for the main flow.
criteria.pass_condition: Main call flow (OPENING + PITCH + DATA_CAPTURE + CLOSE) is under 450 words.
fail_conditions: Main flow exceeds 450 words
Any single section exceeds its guidance range by more than 50%
automated_checks: Count words in OPENING + PITCH + DATA_CAPTURE + CLOSE sections
Flag if total > 450
Flag if any single section is more than 50% over its guidance
criteria.note: This applies to cold outreach scripts. Warm follow-up or scheduled demos may have different limits.

criteria.label: Data Capture Is Clear
criteria.description: If the script is meant to collect information from the prospect (name, email, phone, business details), the DATA_CAPTURE section must clearly define what to ask, how to confirm, and what to do if the prospect declines to provide info.
criteria.pass_condition: Script has DATA_CAPTURE section with explicit fields to collect, confirmation phrasing, and fallback if prospect declines.
required_elements: List of specific fields to collect
Confirmation phrasing (repeat back to confirm accuracy)
Fallback/exit if prospect declines to share
fail_conditions: No DATA_CAPTURE section on a script designed to gather leads
Vague data capture: 'collect their info' without specifying what
No confirmation step (how does AI know it captured correctly?)
No fallback if prospect says 'I don't want to give you that'
automated_checks: Find DATA_CAPTURE section
Check for field labels: name, email, phone, business name, or similar
Check for confirmation phrasing keywords: 'just to confirm', 'let me repeat', 'does that sound right'
Check for fallback phrase in DATA_CAPTURE: 'no problem', 'that's okay', 'I understand'

criteria.label: Graceful Exit if Not Interested
criteria.description: Script must handle disinterest gracefully. One attempt to address the concern is allowed. After that, the AI must close politely and not push further. No aggressive re-engagement loops.
criteria.pass_condition: Script contains GRACEFUL_EXIT section. After one soft re-engagement attempt, if the prospect is still not interested, the AI says thank you and ends the call cleanly.
fail_conditions: No GRACEFUL_EXIT section
Exit involves more than one re-engagement attempt after 'not interested'
Exit is abrupt or rude
Exit still tries to schedule a follow-up after explicit rejection
Exit contains guilt or pressure: 'I just want to make sure you're not missing out'
criteria.acceptable_exit: Thank you so much for your time — I won't take up any more of it. Have a great day!
automated_checks: Find GRACEFUL_EXIT section
Verify section exists and contains closing language
Flag if GRACEFUL_EXIT contains: 'but', 'however', 'just one more thing', 'before I go, can I just'
Flag if GRACEFUL_EXIT loops back to PITCH
Verify graceful exit word count is 20–50 words (short and clean)

criteria.label: No False Claims About Results
criteria.description: Script must not make false or unverifiable claims about PantryMate's results, client outcomes, or competitive advantages. Results may vary. Claims must be framed appropriately.
criteria.pass_condition: All result claims are hedged appropriately. No guaranteed outcomes. No fabricated statistics.
forbidden_claims: The only solution on the market
acceptable_claims: Some of our clients have seen...
In our experience, businesses like yours often find...
We've worked with restaurants that have noticed...
Results vary, but the feedback we get is...
automated_checks: Scan for forbidden_claims patterns
Scan for 'guarantee' within 10 words of result/revenue/income
Scan for '100%' followed by positive outcome claim
Scan for 'every' followed by client/customer/business + positive claim
Flag specific dollar amount claims without 'results may vary'

failures: list of failed criteria with specific line references
